page.content: The table outlines various instruments and assessment areas utilized in a research context, detailing the time of assessments and the individuals applying these instruments. The first instrument, the Sampling form assistant, assesses age, sex, and inclusion/exclusion criteria at Baseline, applied by Research. The second instrument, the Sociodemographic data form assistant, examines age, sex, marital status, and educational level at Baseline, also applied by Research. The table includes a reference to socio-economic group [29] and occupation, but this has no associated instrument or application. The SPPI psychiatric interview, administered by a psychiatrist [30], focuses on psychiatric diagnosis at Baseline, under Research. The Pain catastrophizing scale [20] assistant evaluates the severity of catastrophizing during Baseline and follow-up sessions*, with oversight by Research. The Hamilton test for Depression assistant [31,32] assesses severity of depression at Baseline and follow-up sessions*, managed by Research.

page.content: The Hamilton test for Anxiety assistant [33,34] measures severity of anxiety at Baseline and follow-up sessions*, conducted by Research. The Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire [35,36] assistant reviews general function at Baseline and follow-up sessions*, also in a research setting. The Medical record assistant evaluates pharmacological side-effect events during follow-up sessions*, again under Research. The EQ-5D [37] assistant assesses health-related quality of life at Baseline and follow-up sessions*, facilitated by Research. Lastly, the CSRI [38] assistant reviews health and social services use at Baseline and follow-up sessions*, conducted under Research. Each instrument contributes comprehensive data relevant to the study’s objectives.
